sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

questions.passage: Researchers investigated a freshwater fish population exposed to a pollutant that selects for a detoxifying enzyme allele because allele frequencies change through selection, drift, migration, mutation, and nonrandom mating, but genotype frequencies also depend on assumptions of Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether the detoxifying allele increased because carriers survived better or because sampling error alone shifted the observed frequency. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared three replicate ponds with pollutant exposure, matched unexposed ponds, and a post-exposure breeding generation. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used genotyping of adults before exposure, survivors after exposure, and offspring after random mating among survivors. Figure 1 reports pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. Initial allele frequencies, pond size, and sampling effort were similar across exposed and control ponds. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured enzyme activity by genotype after pollutant challenge, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether genotype-specific survival explained the allele-frequency shift. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: observing Hardy-Weinberg proportions in offspring does not mean selection was absent in the parental survival step. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which interpretation best fits the allele-frequency pattern?

choices.A: Pollutant exposure is associated with increased survival of allele A carriers, followed by near-stable allele frequency in offspring.
choices.B: Allele A disappears after pollutant exposure, proving it is lethal in all environments, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: Control ponds show the same large allele-frequency shift as exposed ponds.
choices.D: Random mating in offspring proves selection never occurred among parents.

explanations.A: Correct. Allele A frequency rises after pollutant exposure but not in matched controls. The relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Selection can act during survival, while random mating can later restore Hardy-Weinberg genotype proportions at the new allele frequency. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: The allele A frequency increases among pollutant survivors. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Control survivors remain close to the starting frequency. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Hardy-Weinberg-like offspring proportions do not erase earlier survival selection. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Frequency of detoxifying allele A before exposure, after survival, and in offspring.
questions.passageHelp:
The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: If p = 0.56 for allele A in the offspring generation, what heterozygote frequency is expected under Hardy-Weinberg assumptions?

choices.A: Approximately q2 = 0.19, because heterozygotes contain only the non-A allele.
choices.C: Approximately p2 = 0.31, because all heterozygotes are counted as AA homozygotes.
choices.D: Exactly 1.00, because random mating makes every fish heterozygous.

explanations.A: q2 gives the aa homozygote frequency. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. Hardy-Weinberg heterozygosity is 2pq. The relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. This calculation uses the offspring allele frequency rather than the starting population frequency. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: p2 gives the AA homozygote frequency, not heterozygotes. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Random mating does not make all offspring heterozygous; genotype frequencies depend on p and q. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

choices.A: They eliminate the need to genotype survivors. under the experimental conditions described
choices.B: They help distinguish pollutant-associated selection from allele-frequency changes caused by sampling or pond conditions alone.
choices.C: They prove that mutation rate is zero at every locus in the genome, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: They force allele A to become recessive in exposed ponds.

explanations.A: Survivor genotyping is necessary to measure allele-frequency change after exposure. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Correct. Control ponds remain near the starting allele frequency. The relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. A matched control is critical for interpreting environmental selection. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.C: Control ponds do not prove a genome-wide absence of mutation. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Dominance is not changed by the existence of a control pond. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

questions.stem: What additional result would most strongly support genotype-specific survival?

choices.A: All genotypes survive equally under pollutant exposure while allele A rises dramatically, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.B: Allele A frequency rises only in unexposed ponds with no pollutant challenge.
choices.C: The detoxifying enzyme is lowest in AA fish despite their highest survival.
choices.D: AA and Aa fish show higher survival and higher detoxifying-enzyme activity than aa fish under pollutant exposure.

explanations.A: Equal survival would not support genotype-specific selection. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: That would weaken a pollutant-selection model. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: That would not connect allele A to detoxifying activity. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. The proposed follow-up measures enzyme activity by genotype after pollutant challenge. The relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Selection claims are stronger when genotype, phenotype, and survival are linked. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.stem: Which conclusion would be too broad?

choices.A: Allele A frequency increased among pollutant survivors.
choices.B: Control ponds help evaluate drift or sampling effects.
choices.C: Heterozygote frequency can be estimated with 2pq after random mating, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.D: Hardy-Weinberg proportions in offspring prove the population experienced no selection during the previous survival step.

explanations.A: That is shown in the exposed survivor stage. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: That is why matched controls were included. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: That follows from Hardy-Weinberg assumptions. Here, the relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. Hardy-Weinberg genotype proportions can occur after allele frequencies have already changed. The relevant evidence is that pollutant-exposed ponds showed increased allele A among survivors and a heterozygote frequency close to 2pq in the offspring generation This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Do not use equilibrium proportions to deny earlier selection without considering timing. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.passage: Researchers investigated families segregating two autosomal markers near a metabolic-disease locus because linkage analysis uses recombination frequency to infer physical proximity, while pedigree pattern helps distinguish dominant, recessive, and sex-linked inheritance. The investigators framed the project around a narrow mechanistic question: whether marker M1 or M2 was more closely linked to the disease locus in informative meioses. Rather than treating the phenotype as a single endpoint, they collected biochemical, cellular, and control measurements so that an apparent association could be separated from a direct pathway effect. The primary experiment compared affected and unaffected family members genotyped at M1 and M2 across three generations. Samples were prepared under matched conditions for cell number, total protein, incubation time, and buffer composition. The main assay used recombination counting between disease status and each marker haplotype in informative parent-offspring transmissions. Figure 1 reports M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children. Values are means from independent preparations, and the error bars in the original analysis were small enough that the ordering of the groups was reproducible across trials. The authors did not use the figure alone as proof of mechanism; instead, they used it to choose which follow-up experiment would best distinguish between competing explanations. Several controls were built into the design. Nonpaternity testing was negative, marker genotyping quality was high, and penetrance was assumed complete for the linkage calculation. These details matter because many superficially attractive interpretations would predict a global change in viability, loading, or nonspecific assay performance rather than a pathway-specific change. The investigators also measured lod-score comparison at different recombination fractions, which allowed them to ask whether the perturbation changed expression, localization, catalytic activity, or signal propagation. A follow-up experiment tested whether a lower recombination fraction made one marker more useful for tracking the disease allele. This experiment was deliberately designed to avoid a common interpretive trap: a marker can be linked to a disease locus without causing the disease itself. They avoided claiming that every downstream physiological effect had been proven, because the study did not include whole-animal outcomes or long-term compensation. The figure provides quantitative evidence about the immediate assay readout, the controls determine whether that readout is technically interpretable, and the follow-up experiment tests whether the proposed mechanism survives an additional perturbation.
questions.stem: Which marker is more closely linked to the disease locus?

choices.A: Both markers are equally linked because each was genotyped in the same family.
choices.B: Neither marker, because linked markers must recombine in all informative meioses, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: M1, because it recombines with the disease haplotype in 2/40 meioses rather than 10/40.
choices.D: M2, because more recombinants always indicate closer physical proximity.

explanations.A: Equal genotyping does not imply equal recombination frequency. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Linked markers recombine less often, not more often. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. M1 has a recombination fraction of 0.05, while M2 has 0.25. The relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Lower recombination frequency indicates a shorter genetic distance from the disease locus. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: A lower recombination fraction indicates closer linkage. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

The researchers concluded that the data supported a specific model, but they noted that the passage evidence was strongest for the step directly measured by the assay.

In interpreting the study, the reader should keep the experimental hierarchy in mind.

Answers that merely name a molecule from the passage are usually weaker than answers that explain how the measured intervention changes the pathway while leaving alternative explanations less consistent with the observations.

A strong answer therefore has to account for the numerical trend, the biological pathway, and the limits of the design.

questions.stem: What inheritance pattern is most consistent with unaffected parents having affected children of both sexes?

choices.A: Autosomal recessive inheritance, assuming complete penetrance and no new mutation.
choices.B: Mitochondrial inheritance, because affected fathers transmit mitochondrial DNA to all children.
choices.C: X-linked dominant inheritance, because unaffected fathers commonly transmit it to all sons.
choices.D: Autosomal dominant inheritance with complete penetrance in every carrier parent.

explanations.A: Correct. The pedigree description includes affected individuals of both sexes and unaffected parents with affected children. The relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Classic inheritance reasoning must be combined with the linkage data rather than replaced by it. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: Mitochondrial DNA is typically maternally inherited, and this passage describes autosomal markers. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: X-linked dominant disorders do not pass father-to-son through the X chromosome. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Unaffected parents having affected children is less consistent with complete-penetrance dominant inheritance. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

choices.A: It converts an autosomal pedigree into a mitochondrial pedigree.
choices.B: It measures the recombination fraction directly by cutting DNA at M1, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: It proves M1 causes the disease rather than marking a nearby locus.
choices.D: It helps ensure apparent recombination events are not actually explained by incorrect family relationships.

explanations.A: Pedigree verification does not change the mode of inheritance. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Nonpaternity testing verifies relationships; it is not a recombination assay. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Relationship testing does not prove causation. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: Correct. Linkage calculations rely on accurate parent-offspring relationships. The relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. A false relationship can create apparent recombinants and distort mapping. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.

questions.stem: Which follow-up would best support M1 as a useful tracking marker?

choices.A: M2 shows a higher recombination fraction and a stronger lod score at theta = 0.50.
choices.B: All genotyping quality controls fail for M1 but not M2.
choices.C: A lod-score peak near recombination fraction 0.05 for M1 is higher than the score for M2.
choices.D: M1 is present only in affected individuals because it is the disease-causing mutation itself.

explanations.A: Theta 0.50 implies unlinked assortment. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.B: Failed genotyping would weaken confidence in M1 linkage. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: Correct. The passage proposes lod-score comparison at different recombination fractions. The relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. LOD analysis formalizes whether observed co-segregation is more likely than no linkage. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.D: The passage describes M1 as a marker, not necessarily causal. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.

choices.A: Because M1 is linked, it must be the causal mutation responsible for the metabolic disease.
choices.B: Unaffected parents with affected children can suggest recessive inheritance, producing the opposite endpoint pattern from the one measured.
choices.C: A marker can help track a nearby disease allele in a family.
choices.D: M1 is closer to the disease locus than M2 by recombination counting.

explanations.A: Correct. A linked marker may be physically near the causal variant without being the variant itself. The relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children This answer fits because it connects the measured result to the relevant biological step rather than relying on a memorized keyword. Linkage is about co-segregation, not automatic molecular causation. It also respects the controls and limitations of the experiment, so the conclusion stays within what was actually tested.
explanations.B: That fits the pedigree description under the stated assumptions. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.C: That is the purpose of linkage mapping. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
explanations.D: That is supported by the lower recombinant count. Here, the relevant evidence is that M1 recombined with the disease haplotype in 2 of 40 informative meioses, whereas M2 recombined in 10 of 40; affected individuals appeared in both sexes and unaffected parents sometimes had affected children The choice would require a pattern that the passage does not report, or it would ignore a control that was included to make the pathway-specific interpretation possible.
